dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Roger Deakins ASC BSC discipline (1917, Blade Runner 2049, Skyfall, Sicario, No Country for Old Men). Medium: ARRI Alexa 65 or film-equivalent digital, 2.39:1 anamorphic. Era: contemporary prestige. Named anchor: Deakins motivated-practical, negative-fill doctrine. Signature light: one single motivated source — practical or natural, visible or implied in-frame; shadow side left genuinely dark into the black point, NO secondary fill unless bounced off a visible surface. Color/grade: earth-natural gamut — umber, ochre, olive, warm stone — never teal-orange; single warm accent from sodium, tungsten, or fire; ARRI LOG-C delivered clean without overcooking. Lens/optics: Zeiss Master Anamorphic 40mm–65mm primes, f/2.8–f/5.6, subtle horizontal streak flare from practicals at bright sources, 35mm-equivalent shadow grain present but not gritty. Texture/render: photoreal with painterly restraint — grain lives in shadows, skin holds texture, no digital-smooth artifice. Composition+motion: locked-off or slow deliberate dolly 2–4 seconds across frame; composition breathes — subject small in environment, foreground frame element anchors depth, negative space carries weight; cuts are rare and reverent.
anchor: Roger Deakins ASC motivated-practical key, shadow side into the black point, ARRI Alexa 65, 50mm anamorphic, earth-umber grade, locked-off slow dolly, one source no fill.
use: Drops a Deakins naturalist light doctrine onto the world — single motivated source, deep shadow, photoreal grain — without overwriting the world's geometry or palette; use when the world needs cinematographic authority and shadow-side credibility.
avoid: NO Ryan Gosling K, NO Rick Deckard, NO James Bond, NO Sam Mendes cast names, NO named Deakins-shot characters or locations (1917 trench, Blade Runner 2049 Los Angeles). NO teal-orange grade excess. NO fill-flash flat lighting. NO handheld shake. NO neon, NO fluorescent green. NO overcorrected digital-clean look.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Stanley Kubrick precision-geometry doctrine (The Shining, 2001, Eyes Wide Shut, Full Metal Jacket, A Clockwork Orange). Medium: 65mm Mitchell BNC or Panavision anamorphic equivalent, 1.33:1 to 1.85:1 aspect. Era: timeless institutional. Named anchor: Kubrick one-point corridor, forward dolly symmetry. Signature light: cold institutional practicals — fluorescent tubes, single overhead source, practical corridor lighting; light is functional, not dramatic; fills the frame evenly so nothing hides; the architecture IS the threat. Color/grade: desaturated institutional palette — cream walls, grey carpet, cold white fluorescent bias; single off-key accent (red carpet, yellow jacket) placed as compositional instrument. Lens/optics: Zeiss Super Speed 18–25mm wide prime or Kubrick's custom Nasa f/0.7 wide, f/2–f/4; ceiling visible in almost every frame; slight wide-angle barrel adds weight to architecture; no lens flare. Texture/render: meticulous photoreal, every surface chosen; patterned geometry (carpet, ceiling tile, linoleum) repeats into vanishing point, reinforcing perspective pressure. Composition+motion: one-point perspective corridor, vanishing line dead center; slow forward dolly 4–8 seconds per shot creating approach-dread; human figure small, centered, architecture towers; ceiling weight deliberate; every element placed on a grid.
anchor: Kubrick one-point corridor, vanishing line dead center, wide prime ceiling-visible, cold institutional light, slow forward dolly, human small in architecture, geometric carpet to vanishing point.
use: Imposes Kubrick's symmetry-and-architecture grammar — one-point vanishing line, ceiling weight, cold even light, the subject dwarfed by designed space — as a compositional and lighting layer on the chosen world.
avoid: NO named Kubrick film characters (Jack Torrance, HAL 9000, Alex DeLarge, named Kubrick cast). NO hotel-twin-girls image. NO monolith prop shape. NO franchise costumes. NO handheld, NO dutch angle, NO rack focus pull, NO shallow bokeh dominant.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Denis Villeneuve / Roger Deakins / Greig Fraser scale-dread doctrine (Dune, Arrival, Blade Runner 2049, Sicario). Medium: ARRI Alexa 65, IMAX 65mm for monument shots, 2.39:1. Era: contemporary prestige science-fiction and thriller. Named anchor: Villeneuve monolith scale grammar, silhouette-speck composition. Signature light: dust-veiled sun or overcast atmosphere that diffuses the key into a wall of pale light — no hard-edge shadows on environment; only the human silhouette reads a sharp edge against the haze; sub-atmosphere halos mass without detail. Color/grade: near-monochrome sand-dust or concrete-fog palette — warm grey-beige in dune environments, cold blue-grey in arrival/urban environments; saturation pulled toward desaturated except single warm accent (sodium flare, a torch, a ship window). Lens/optics: 65mm Master Anamorphic or IMAX spherical 65mm; f/5.6–f/8 deep focus so environment holds full resolution while subject reads only as silhouette scale; horizontal anamorphic bokeh on any out-of-focus practical. Texture/render: atmosphere-dense photoreal — grain, particulate, haze all physically present and volumetric. Composition+motion: tiny human silhouette at 10–15% of frame height placed against the monolith mass occupying 60–80% of vertical; slow ship or object drift across otherwise locked frame; fog-wall reveal dolly 3–5 seconds; brutalist negative space between elements deliberate.
anchor: Villeneuve monolith scale: silhouette speck at 10% frame height, dust-veiled pale-haze key, ARRI 65mm deep focus, brutalist negative space, slow object drift across locked frame.
use: Injects Villeneuve's scale-dread grammar — tiny human against monolithic mass, atmospheric diffusion dissolving detail, sub-bass compositional stillness — as a light and composition layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named Villeneuve characters or franchise sigils (sandworm, ornithopter, heptapod shell, K's Joi). NO named films or IP locations. NO shallow bokeh dominant over the subject. NO warm saturation bloom, NO Dutch angle, NO handheld.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Emmanuel 'Chivo' Lubezki AMC naturalist doctrine (The Revenant, Birdman, Children of Men, Tree of Life, Gravity). Medium: ARRI Alexa or RED, 1.85:1 or 2.39:1, paired with wide primes. Era: contemporary prestige auteur. Named anchor: Lubezki natural-light-only, magic-hour golden bias, handheld micro-drift. Signature light: natural sources ONLY — sun, moon, fire, candle, window skylight; no artificial fixture visible or compositionally present; magic hour and blue hour are the default operating register (low sun, long golden shadows, raking warm-orange key); shadow fill comes from sky bounce (cool blue), never from a fill light rig. Color/grade: golden-earth dominant — umber skin tones, warm straw and sienna in sunlit areas; blue-hour scenes shift to cool cerulean shadow, protected warm highlights; no teal-orange grade; organic film grain throughout all values. Lens/optics: wide primes at 14mm, 16mm, 21mm, 35mm; f/5.6–f/8 deep focus throughout; slight uncorrected barrel distortion at frame edge; natural highlight bloom (no added diffusion filter — the atmosphere does it); no lens flare added artificially. Texture/render: organic, alive, skin and foliage hold sub-pixel texture; grain is natural silver-halide character, not digital noise. Composition+motion: handheld with intentional micro-drift and organic breath — not shaky war-doc, not dead-stabilized, but human-alive pressure; camera weaves through space following action in long uninterrupted arcs; reframes natural, not choreographed.
anchor: Lubezki AMC natural-light only, magic-hour golden key, handheld micro-drift on wide prime, sky-bounce cool fill, deep-focus earth-umber grade, long-take organic arc.
use: Applies Lubezki's natural-light-only, wide-lens handheld doctrine — magic-hour golden rake, organic micro-drift, sky-bounce fill — as a light and camera layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named Lubezki characters (Hugh Glass, Riggan Thomson, Theo Faron). NO Revenant frontier wilderness specifically. NO handheld shake (micro-drift is alive, not chaotic). NO artificial light source added. NO teal-orange Hollywood grade. NO rack-focus pull from subject to background. NO fill flash.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Sony Pictures Animation Spider-Verse hybrid doctrine (Into the Spider-Verse, Across the Spider-Verse, Beyond the Spider-Verse). Medium/era: 2018–2025 digital animation hybrid — 3D-modeled forms shaded with 2D-comic surface grammar. Named anchor: Spider-Verse Ben-Day halftone, dual-cadence 12fps hero / 24fps camera. Signature light: bold motivated key from a single graphic direction — fill is a hard-stepped complementary color, not a gradient; rim light in accent hue becomes a flat graphic shape; highlights are graphic opaque white shapes, not specular falloff. Color/grade: high-saturation primary comic palette — deep violet shadow, saturated red and cyan mid, electric yellow accent; deliberately NOT naturalistic; Ben-Day halftone dot texture scales with distance (larger dots on background). Lens/optics: 35–50mm panel framing treated as comic frame; chromatic aberration split 2–4px cyan-magenta at high-contrast edges (print mis-registration, not defect); no lens flare unless drawn as a starburst graphic shape. Texture/render: bold black brush-textured line 3–6px, weight heavier on shadow-side; color fill layer sits 1–2px offset from line layer (old-comic printing misregistration); ink texture on line visible. Composition+motion: graphic panel-frame compositions; hero character animated 12fps on 2s holds while camera and background move 24fps; speed lines on fast action drawn as graphic streaks; freeze-frame allowed at peak beat.
anchor: Spider-Verse Ben-Day halftone texture, 12fps hero on 2s / 24fps camera dual-cadence, bold 4px brush-textured black line, cyan-magenta print-misregistration split, primary comic palette.
use: Layers Spider-Verse's halftone-comic hybrid grammar — printed dot texture, dual-cadence animation, chromatic aberration as mis-registration, graphic-shape light — onto the chosen world's geometry and cast.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Fortiche Production painterly-3D doctrine (Arcane season 1 and 2). Medium/era: 2021–2024 Fortiche hybrid — 3D-modeled forms surface-shaded with oil-paint texture hand-applied at every silhouette edge. Named anchor: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D surface treatment — NOT anime, NOT cel — paint stroke IS the shading method. Signature light: motivated single-direction key; bounce fill in complementary hue (if key is teal, fill is magenta-pink; if key is warm amber, fill is cool violet); rim light is always an accent color, NEVER white; hard-black ambient occlusion forbidden — AOcclusion is painterly-soft in shadow-hue, not black. Color/grade: Fortiche Vision3 500T-derived curve — warm-highlight, cool-shadow, painterly halation on hot pixels; deep-violet for the darkest shadow value (NO pure black anywhere); magenta-pink + teal two-color system in the painted shadows and rims. Lens/optics: 35–50mm, f/2.8–f/4; Vision3 500T halation on specular and emissive sources; 35mm-equivalent grain present in every value step, not in the highlights only. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at every silhouette edge; skin has hand-painted blush and pore work, NOT subsurface-scattering smooth; hair masses show individual paint-stroke separation, not smooth spec; highlights are painted-in decisions, not PBR physical math. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with hand-animated painted smear frames at peak action (2–3 frames, motion-streak painted); 1.5–2s emotional hold; slow-in slow-out with weight; no motion-blur.
anchor: Fortiche painterly-3D: oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal+magenta two-color bounce, Vision3 500T halation, smear frames painted not motion-blurred.
use: Applies Fortiche's impasto 2D-over-3D surface grammar — painted edges, violet shadow, complementary-hue fill, halation — as a texture and light layer over the chosen world's forms.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the WIT Studio / MAPPA scale-dread military doctrine. Medium/era: 2013–2023 Japanese television animation, wide-aspect scale composition. Named anchor: WIT/MAPPA colossal-wall gravity, near-monochrome military desaturation. Signature light: overcast grey-green diffuse ambient key — NO warm bounce, NO dramatic motivated source; torch or signal flare provides the only local hard source in interiors, reading as a single muted signal-accent against the grey field; shadow is flat and heavy, communicating institutional dread rather than filmic drama. Color/grade: near-monochrome grey-green military desaturation throughout — all secondary hues pulled toward olive-grey; one muted signal-color accent per composition (a flare, a cloak trim, a warning light); NO warmth, NO saturation bloom. Lens/optics: wide establishing 24–28mm simulated for scale-dread compositions placing human figures as scale indicators; tighter 50mm for survival-detail human beats; 16:9 or 2.35:1 for maximum vertical scale impact. Texture/render: confident 2–3px black line, desaturated 2–3 value cel shading; texture emphasis on weathered stone and coarse fabric over clean color fill; environment has surface weathering and staining baked into mid-tones. Composition+motion: scale-dread composition bias — wall, mass, or architectural element occupying 70–85% of vertical frame; human figure small at base or mid-frame as scale indicator; near-stillness as motion grammar; one atmospheric shift only (wind through dust, distant silhouette crossing, flare igniting); camera holds, tension hold 2–3s.
anchor: WIT/MAPPA colossal-wall grammar: near-monochrome grey-green military, one muted signal-accent, wide 24mm scale-dread framing, human as scale indicator at base, stillness and dread.
use: Imposes WIT/MAPPA's scale-dread grammar — near-monochrome military desaturation, colossal-mass framing, stillness as tension — as a composition and lighting layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Toei Animation open-sea shonen bold-cel doctrine (Toei TV production, Wano arc onward into theatrical quality). Medium/era: 2000s–2020s Toei bold-outline cel, elevated to Wano-era filmic quality. Named anchor: Toei frog-eye low-angle + grand-horizon sky drama. Signature light: the sky is the light engine — gold-cream on adventure daylight beats, amber-orange flare at emotional peaks; characters lit from the sky-and-sun angle; shadow is a single hard cel step in deep saturated marine-blue, NEVER grey or black; no fill, no bounce — all light is atmospheric and primary. Color/grade: Toei adventure primaries at full saturation — marine-blue deep shadow, primary-yellow mid key, primary-red accent; cream-warm highlight; NO pastel, NO desaturation, NO neon; open sea reads deep teal-navy, not resort turquoise. Lens/optics: flat 2D, simulated frog-eye low angle from knee-height default — steep perspective, horizon low in the bottom quarter of the frame; wide comedy reactions push into fish-eye distortion; 50mm portrait for quiet emotional beats; NO lens flare (flat 2D world). Texture/render: bold 3–5px pure black outline, uniform weight, no taper; silhouette-first; fill is flat cel in 2 value steps only; anatomy follows Toei adventure grammar (broad shoulders, expressive distortion at peak emotion, elastic physics). Composition+motion: frog-eye from knee to chest height creating upward thrust; oversized horizon composition with subject filling mid-frame, bold sky filling 60%+ of frame; smear frame on peak physical action (single multi-ghost limb frame); freeze on reaction shot 24–36 frames; wind-led camera rhythm for travel.
anchor: Toei bold-cel frog-eye from knee height, oversized gold-amber sky filling 60% of frame, deep marine-blue shadow, bold 4px uniform black outline, flat 2-value cel, no desaturation.
use: Applies Toei's bold-cel frog-eye sky-drama grammar — low-angle upward thrust, adventure-primary palette, oversized horizon — as an energy and composition layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Studio Pierrot shinobi-action doctrine. Medium/era: 2000s–2017 Kishimoto-canon Japanese television animation, elevated at key arc climaxes. Named anchor: Pierrot chakra-arc inward coil, determination golden warm. Signature light: warm golden-orange key from sun-through-canopy or village lantern; deep forest-green ambient shadow fill; dust and particulate in sun shafts; when a chakra energy arc fires, it acts as its own brief warm-rim coiling inward — NOT a bloom flooding the frame, just a coiling warm edge light that dissipates into the settle. Color/grade: warm golden-orange determination dominant; deep forest-green secondary; warm dust-trail accents; NO cold desaturation, NO pastel, NO neon. Lens/optics: flat 2D, simulated 35–50mm portrait for character beats, wider 24mm establishing for training-ground or confrontation-distance shots; 16:9 aspect; chakra coil visualized as a flat graphical arc, not a volumetric glow. Texture/render: confident 2–3px black line, uniform weight at rest, single smear frame on peak physical effort; Kishimoto paint-block brows set in determination; costume grammar is utility-functional — bandage wraps, utility flak vests, open-toe sandal silhouette — every piece chosen for silhouette readability. Composition+motion: confident hold at rest; taijutsu snap with single smear frame; at climax one inward chakra spiral coils around fist or feet, leaf-litter lifting in vortex; determined final hold 1.0–1.2s; run-up reads as arc approach — body low, arms back, dust trailing.
anchor: Pierrot shinobi chakra-arc: warm golden-orange sun key, deep forest-green shadow, inward coil as self-generated rim, determination hold, single smear on taijutsu peak, dust-trail run-up arc.
use: Layers Pierrot's shinobi-determination grammar — warm golden chakra arc as rim, forest-green shadow, functional costume silhouette, taijutsu smear — as a motion and light layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the ufotable 2D-cel-over-3D-background compositing doctrine. Medium/era: 2019–2024 ufotable hi-fi composite. Named anchor: ufotable elemental breath-path — clean blade arc as luminous ribbon written in atmosphere, NOT a named power. Signature light: motivated single-direction key with volumetric god-rays (visible particulate atmosphere always present); warm rim from key-side, cool-slate shadow ambient; effects layer generates its own light (water arc reflects sky, fire arc glows warm on character face); every particle in the air is lit from its own physics. Color/grade: cool blue-slate shadow, warm fire-amber or teal water accent, cream-warm highlight — balanced and restrained; NO neon, NO flat desaturation; effects layer is the only saturated chromatic event against a restrained background. Lens/optics: simulated 40–85mm portrait to wide; 2.35:1 letterbox option; f/2.8–f/4 shallow on hero, f/8 wide on environment; bloom only on light-source objects (not on characters); NO lens flare abuse. Texture/render: crisp 2px black line on character, uniform weight, no taper; 3-value cel fill — cel character over hand-painted-over 3D background that reads luminous and painterly; effects animation is full 24fps with single-frame smear at peak, particle settle at end. Composition+motion: blade arc as reading line — the ribbon path of the breath must trace a legible geometric shape (curve, helix, flat wave) that the eye follows from initiation to terminus; character hold 2–3s after settle; particle drift continues after arc completes.
anchor: Ufotable breath-arc grammar: crisp 2px cel character over hand-painted luminous 3D background, clean blade ribbon path, volumetric god-rays, cool-slate shadow, effects as self-lit particles, 2.35:1.
use: Applies ufotable's cel-over-3D compositing grammar — luminous painted background, clean arc ribbon, volumetric atmosphere, effects as self-light — as a rendering and light layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Hayao Miyazaki hand-drawn nature-organic doctrine (pastoral-fantasy grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 1984–2023 hand-drawn theatrical animation. Named anchor: Miyazaki-lineage wind-mass motion — leaves, hair, fabric, grass all move in independent slow-different-phase rhythms, communicating living air. Signature light: soft diffuse natural key from sky or window — no hard shadow edge anywhere; the light wraps; interior warmth from lantern or hearth as secondary source, always with a hand-painted bloom halo; every light source has a hand-painted softness character that differs from the next. Color/grade: warm organic palette — terracotta, muted sage, warm ochre, sky-grey-blue; colors aged and mixed on the brush, not pre-selected hex swatches; ink on backgrounds is gouache layered with visible ridges; NO photorealistic texture, NO smooth CG surface; character skin has hand-painted blush, warm shadow under chin and eye. Lens/optics: flat 2D, simulated wide 28–40mm with low horizon for sky drama; 1.85:1 or academy 1.33:1 aspect; NO depth-of-field blur — Miyazaki-lineage holds every plane in focus simultaneously; optical sharpness is a function of paint quality, not lens simulation. Texture/render: linework is hand-inked with subtle variation in weight — NOT a uniform computer stroke; the line feels drawn; background gouache has directional brush-ridge texture at close read; hair flows as a unified mass that breaks apart at the tip-ends. Composition+motion: foreground grass or foliage overlaps frame edge; sky fills 40–60% of frame for nature scenes; wind moves things in slow 1–2 second cycles across different planes independently (parallax is hand-drawn, not 3D); characters walk with characteristic slow-drag weight — foot strikes the ground on the down-beat, hair and skirt trail 6–8 frames behind.
anchor: Miyazaki-lineage hand-drawn organic: wind moves every plane independently at different phase, hand-inked line weight variation, gouache background with visible brush ridges, all planes in focus, slow heel-strike walk with trailing fabric.
use: Layers Miyazaki-lineage's hand-drawn organic nature grammar — independent wind phases, gouache background texture, all-in-focus, trailing fabric weight — as a rendering and motion layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.

dna: Stylized animation editing in the Satoshi Kon shape-fold grammar (Perfect Blue, Millennium Actress, Paprika, Paranoia Agent, Tokyo Godfathers). Medium/era: 1997–2006 Madhouse theatrical/OVA animation, precise editorial animation. Named anchor: Kon shape-match cut — the final shape in frame A becomes the opening shape in frame B; the cut IS the storytelling. Signature light: no dominant light style — Kon's lighting serves the match-cut logic; scene-fold transitions must carry consistent value-structure across the cut so the eye reads the shape-echo before registering the scene change; interior fluorescent, exterior overcast, memory-golden are the three operating light registers depending on layer (reality / memory / dream). Color/grade: high-fidelity Tokyo urban palette for reality-layer (cool neon-tinged night, fluorescent interior flat, concrete grey overcast); memory-layer shifts to warm sepia-amber; dream-layer is hyper-saturated impossible-color; the shift in grade IS the layer signal. Lens/optics: simulated 35–50mm; depth of field present and used to separate layers (sharp in current layer, soft-dreamy in memory/dream layer); NO handheld; camera moves are authored and purposeful, never observational. Texture/render: Madhouse-quality cel linework; crowd animation is dense and observationally specific (individual behavior, not repeating crowds); urban backgrounds are architecturally specific (Tokyo detail) but originals only. Composition+motion: aspect-echo compositions — the geometric proportion of subject-to-negative-space in frame A must mirror frame B; hard cut on gesture peak (hand raise, door open, object thrown) where the gesture continues in the new scene; screen-direction carries across the cut; waking overlap: the final 4–6 frames of a dream sequence overlay with the opening of the waking scene.
anchor: Satoshi Kon shape-match cut: frame A's closing shape becomes frame B's opening shape, hard cut on gesture peak, waking overlap 4–6 frames, dense observational crowd, aspect-echo compositions across the cut.
use: Applies Kon's editorial shape-fold grammar — shape-match on cut, waking overlap, aspect-echo, hard cut on gesture — as a transition and editing logic layer over the chosen world.
avoid: NO named Kon films or their specific scenes. NO named Kon characters (Mima, Chiyoko, Paprika, Conan). NO psychological horror imagery from Kon's films. NO franchise posters or TV-show imagery from Perfect Blue. NO repeating-clone crowd (must be individual behavior). NO smooth glossy modern anime sheen.
